title: CF-NATIVE Hosted TEST Validation
description: Execute the governed hosted TEST validation contract against the Cloudflare-native finalist using Workers, D1, and R2 with synthetic fixtures, TEST-only authority, explicit teardown, and complete evidence capture.
acceptance_criteria: Provider TEST identity, TEST credentials, and teardown authority are available without using production credentials
A non-production CF-NATIVE runtime is provisioned under the zero-cost boundary or execution stops before cost is incurred
Synthetic canonical fixtures enter only through governed import paths
Namespace, tenant, subject, domain, audience, and execution-context isolation pass in the hosted environment
Transaction interruption, duplicate delivery, exact retry, rollback, recovery, export, import, and deterministic reconstruction pass
Provider logs and telemetry preserve Enki privacy and disclosure boundaries
Failure injection covers D1, R2, runtime, credential, quota, and teardown failure paths without manufacturing success
The environment is destroyed and the final TEST state is reconstructable from governed exports and receipts
blocked_reason: Hosted execution requires Cloudflare TEST identity, TEST credentials, and teardown authority. Those external capabilities are not available through the current GitHub execution connection and cannot be inferred or replaced with production credentials.
